evidence.title: Chico superdelegate responds to Kamala Harris VP announcement
evidence.description: As the country reacts to presidential hopeful Joe Biden's pick for a running mate, a Chico democratic superdelegate is making his voice heard. KRCR caught up with campaign strategist Bob Mulholland outside his Chico home, on Tuesday, where he says Kamala Harris is the right choice for Biden. He believes she will help democrats win Pennsylvania, Wisconsin, and Michigan, all states the democrats frequently won until the 2016 election.
evidence.text:
Chico superdelegate responds to Kamala Harris VP announcement
CHICO, Calif. — As the country reacts to presidential hopeful Joe Biden's pick for a running mate, a Chico democratic superdelegate is making his voice heard.
KRCR caught up with campaign strategist Bob Mulholland outside his Chico home, on Tuesday, where he says Kamala Harris is the right choice for Biden.
He believes she will help democrats win Pennsylvania, Wisconsin, and Michigan, all states the democrats frequently won until the 2016 election.
He adds that her status as a woman, child of immigrants, and longstanding political career will help Biden in the path to the presidency.
"She is a former attorney general of California, former prosecutor, and a U.S. senator,” says Mulholland. “In the era of social media, you got to have someone who looks good on television and is articulate and intelligent, and that fits Kamala Harris."
Mulholland said democrats picked up seven congressional seats in 2018 and that Tuesday's announcement could help them keep those seats moving forward.
He predicts that if Biden wins the presidency this November, another woman would replace her as California senator.
Biden’s campaign announced his decision to pick Harris as his running mate earlier Tuesday.

evidence.title: Bob Mulholland, longtime Democratic strategist and Vietnam veteran, dies at 78
evidence.description: KRCR News Channel 7 and KCVU Fox 20 offers local and national news, sports, and weather forecasts to viewers in the Northstate including Redding, Shasta Lake, Shingletown, Anderson, Red Bluff, Corning, Chico, and Oroville. Coverage is also provided on the North Coast through KBVU Fox 28 and KAEF ABC 23.

evidence.title: Late Vietnam veteran, political strategist Bob Mulholland honored in Chico escort ceremony
evidence.description: The Patriot Guard Riders and Veterans Honor Guard escorted late-Chico Democratic Party Strategist and Vietnam Veteran Bob Mulholland from Chico to Sacramento on
evidence.text:
Late Vietnam veteran, political strategist Bob Mulholland honored in Chico escort ceremony
CHICO, Calif. — The Patriot Guard Riders and Veterans Honor Guard escorted late-Chico Democratic Party Strategist and Vietnam Veteran Bob Mulholland from Chico to Sacramento on Monday morning ahead of his funeral at the Arlington National Cemetery in Virginia on Friday, January 23.
Vietnam Vets of America, Chapter 582 President Warren Roll told KRCR that there was a brief moment of remembrance before Mulholland was loaded onto a hearse, with escort assistance from Chico Police, Glenn County and others.
Mulholland passed in October of 2025 and is remembered for his veteran advocacy work in the Northstate and beyond. Roll says Mulholland was an excellent representative of the Democratic Party and had connections with prominent party members.
"He was a very good politician, and he'd go for us to the [Democratic] National Convention back east. He advocated for the veterans and just for how we could help them, which is what our chapter does. We do things for the common good. Any way he could help out, he would help," Roll said.
There are upcoming events to remember Mulholland's life and legacy, including a gathering at the Silver Dollar Speedway's Harvest Hall in Chico on February 21 at 1 p.m.
Additionally, Roll said there are two other events scheduled in Sacramento and Los Angeles later this year.
Mulholland was 78 years old and is survived by his wife, Jane Dolan.
--
